---
title: "Getting Started with AI Tools"
subtitle: "AI Bootcamp for UVM Economics — Session 1"
author:
  - Emily Beam
  - Erkmen Aslim
date: 2026-04-22
institute: "Department of Economics, University of Vermont"
format:
  revealjs: default
  beamer: default
---

# Welcome

## What is this?

Two lunch sessions on AI tools for teaching and research.

- **Today**: Getting started — what AI can do, live demos
- **April 27**: Research pipelines, web scraping, automated grading

No installation required. Watch, ask questions, try things later.

# The AI Ladder

## Where are you?

| Level | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| 0 | Haven't tried it |
| 1 | Tried it once, wasn't impressed |
| 2 | Use occasionally for simple tasks |
| 3 | Regular user, integrated into workflow |
| 4 | Power user, multi-step workflows |
| 5 | Building systems with AI |

**Goal today:** Show you what Levels 3–4 look like.

## The tools landscape

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Chat interfaces**

- ChatGPT, Claude.ai, Copilot
- Good for: quick questions, drafting
- Limit: copy-paste in/out
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Terminal / Code tools**

- Claude Code, GitHub Copilot
- Good for: file access, iteration, pipelines
- Limit: steeper learning curve
:::
::::

UVM provides [Microsoft Copilot](https://go.uvm.edu/copilot) free to everyone.

# Demo 1: Terminal + Claude Code (Emily)

## What you're seeing

1. **Ghostty** — a terminal app (any terminal works)
2. **Claude Code** — AI assistant in the terminal
3. Type a request → AI responds → iterate

Key ideas:

- AI can **read and write files** on your computer
- **Iterate**: "Make it shorter," "Add a section on X"
- Output is **Markdown** — readable as plain text or rendered nicely

## Context and iteration

**Context window**: The AI only "sees" what you show it. Be explicit.

**Iteration tips:**

- First output is a draft, not a final product
- Refine: "Change the tone," "Add an example," "This is wrong because..."
- Save to a file so you can pick up later

**When it's wrong:** AI makes confident-sounding mistakes. Always review output, especially numbers and citations.

# Demo 2: Desktop App (Erkmen)

## Desktop AI tools

<!-- Erkmen: replace this slide -->

- ChatGPT / Claude desktop interface
- When to use chat vs. terminal
- Quick demo

# Demo 3: Teaching Application (Erkmen)

## AI for teaching tasks

<!-- Erkmen: replace this slide -->

- Creating assignment prompts
- Building grading rubrics
- Iterating on rubric criteria

# Wrap-up

## Before next time (April 27)

- Try using an AI tool for **one real task** this week
- Think of a research or teaching task you'd like to automate
- Install guide: [eabeam.github.io/uvmecon-ai/prep.html](https://eabeam.github.io/uvmecon-ai/prep.html)

**Next session:** Research pipelines, web scraping, automated grading

## Resources

- Bootcamp site: [eabeam.github.io/uvmecon-ai](https://eabeam.github.io/uvmecon-ai)
- Claude Code docs: [docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code)
- UVM Copilot (free): [go.uvm.edu/copilot](https://go.uvm.edu/copilot)
- Prompt guide: [docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-engineering/overview](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-engineering/overview)
